statement.p: Suppose that a large pump can empty a swimming pool in 49\ {\rm hr} and that a small pump can empty the same pool in 59\ {\rm hr}. If both pumps are used at the same time, how long will it take to empty the pool?
If both pumps are used at the same time, it will take to empty the pool.

solution.p: Lets understand how to use fractions to model work problems. Assume it takes a pipe 5 hours to empty a pool. If we treat the whole job as one whole, each hour, the pipe would empty \frac{1}{5} of the pool.
Similarly, if it takes a pipe x hours to empty a pool, each hour, the pipe would empty \frac{1}{x} of the pool.
Now we are ready to model this situation with math.
Its given that it takes the large pump 49 hours to empty the pool. Each hour, the large pump empties \frac{1}{49} of the pool.
Similarly, each hour, the smaller pump empties \frac{1}{59} of the pool.
Assume that, if both pipes are turned on, it would take x hours to empty the pool. Thus, each hour, they can empty \frac{1}{x} of the pool.
Now we can write an equation and solve it:
In the second step, to get rid of all fractions, we multiplied each term with the common denominator {2891x}.
By the solution we got, if both pipe are turned on, it would take {26.7685\ {\rm hr}} hours to empty the pool.
